seoTitle: Sign Out of Microsoft Teams on Every Device
metaDescription: Force a global sign-out of Microsoft Teams across every device using the Sign Out Everywhere security option, for personal or work accounts.
intro: Losing a device or responding to a security concern often means you need to kill every active Teams session at once rather than logging out of each app individually. This guide explains how to trigger a global sign-out using Microsoft's account security tools.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar and select View Account to open your Microsoft account management panel.

steps.heading: Go to security settings
steps.body: Navigate to the Security info or My Sign-Ins section of your account console.

steps.heading: Find Advanced Security Options
steps.body: Open the Advanced Security Options category to access session-level controls.

steps.heading: Sign out everywhere
steps.body: Scroll to the Sign Out Everywhere section, click the action button, and confirm the prompt to revoke tokens on every connected device.

steps.heading: Handle enterprise accounts separately
steps.body: For work accounts, an administrator can sign out all sessions for a user through Active Users in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center.

faq.question: Will signing out everywhere delete my messages or files?
faq.answer: No, this only terminates active sessions and revokes tokens — it does not delete your chat history or files.

faq.question: Can I do this myself for a work account, or do I need IT?
faq.answer: For enterprise-managed accounts, a system administrator typically needs to force the sign-out through the admin center.

faq.question: Do I need to clear my app cache after signing out everywhere?
faq.answer: Yes, clearing your local device cache helps speed up a clean sign-in screen after the global logout.

faq.question: Does this work the same for personal and enterprise Microsoft accounts?
faq.answer: The overall goal is the same, but personal accounts use the web-based security portal while enterprise accounts route through the admin center.
